arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Southampton - Sat, Dec 7th 2024, 15:00 Aston Villa will look to make it back-to-back Premier League wins on Saturday afternoon, when Southampton travel to Villa Park (ko 3pm). Unai Emery's men returned to winning ways in B6 on Wednesday evening, with goals from Morgan Rogers, Ollie Watkins and Matty Cash securing a 3-1 victory over Brentford. Villa now hope to further close the gap on the Premier League's top four, in their penultimate home outing before Christmas. Russell Martin's Saints have endured a difficult start to their first season back in the top flight, currently sitting bottom of the table on five points, having won just once. The south coast outfit enter the clash having lose three of their last four, most recently a 5-1 defeat at home to Chelsea in midweek. The Villans will be looking to maintain a positive recent run of form against Southampton, in which they have won each of the last three meetings between the two sides - scoring six and conceding none - and four of the last five. Stats Aston Villa striker Ollie Watkins has been involved in 10 goals in his last five Premier League appearances against promoted clubs, scoring four and assisting six. Adam Armstrong scored in his first ever away Premier League appearance for Southampton in August 2021 against Everton but has since gone 32 away games without scoring, having 51 shots with an expected goals total of 4.7 without finding the net across these matches. Aston Villa are unbeaten in all 11 of their Premier League games against promoted sides under Unai Emery (W9 D2), winning five of their six at home under the Spaniard (D1). Southampton are the only side yet to score a first half away goal in the Premier League this season – the Saints have also had the fewest shots (29) and fewest shots on target (5) in the first 45 minutes of away games of any side this campaign. Aston Villa are unbeaten in their last six home Premier League matches (W3 D3) and have scored 85 goals in 39 home games under Unai Emery, the fourth-most home goals since Emery’s first game in charge at Villa Park in November 2022. Southampton are winless in their last 14 Premier League away games (D3 L11), since a 1-0 win at Chelsea in February 2023. Indeed, no side has won fewer points on the road in the top-flight this term than Saints (1). Aston Villa have won five of their last eight Premier League matches against sides starting the day bottom of the league (D2 L1), though their one defeat in this run was at Villa Park, losing 1-0 to West Ham United in August 2022. Ollie Watkins has scored five goals in his last six home Premier League appearances and has 36 goals overall at Villa Park – the only Aston Villa player with more home goals than Watkins is Dwight Yorke (37). Having been unbeaten in six consecutive away league games against Aston Villa between 2013 and 2020, Southampton have lost their last two visits by an aggregate score of 5-0. Aston Villa have won their last three Premier League games against Southampton (all without conceding), more than they had in their previous 13 against them (W2 D3 L8). The Last Meeting Villa secured a third-consecutive away win in the Premier League the last time the two sides met, as Ollie Watkins' 77th-minute header sealed a 1-0 victory at St Mary's. The hosts had their moments in Hampshire, with Kyle Walker-Peters and James Ward-Prowse both seeing goals disallowed either side of the half-time break. But it was Villa's number 11 who came up trumps for the visitors, heading home Douglas Luiz's free-kick 13 minutes from time to send his side home with all three points. The win secured a second-straight victory for Unai Emery's side, while ending the hosts' run of three wins in a row. Team News Unai Emery remained coy on team news in his pre-match press conference on Friday, insisting he will make a late call on multiple players following Wednesday's win over Brentford. Amadou Onana will face a late fitness test as he closes in on a return from injury, but Jacob Ramsey remains sidelined for the next few weeks. Russell Martin will be boosted by the return of three key players from suspension at Villa Park, as Taylor Harwood-Bellis, Flynn Downes and Tyler Dibling were all forced to sit out in midweek. Jack Stephens will however be suspended, having been sent off against Chelsea, while Jan Bednarek and Paul Onuachu are both doubts for the clash. Managers' Pre-Match Thoughts "I know with my experiences, if we are not being organised in our press, we can struggle, playing against a team like Southampton. They are not getting points like they deserve, because they have not a lot of points, but they deserve more, how they are playing, but they are being very, very brave with the ball." - Unai Emery "They are a very, very good team. Unai Emery has done an amazing job there. You have to accept and appreciate what they did last season was an incredible achievement." - Russell Martin

arts.ttl: Barkley starts, Mings decision - Aston Villa's starting XI vs Southampton predicted - John Townley - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: Matty Cash scored his first league goal in 15 months in the win and is expected to continue at right-back, but Ian Maatsen may come into the side and replace Lucas Digne considering Villa play RB Leipzig on Tuesday night. Aston Villa predicted XI: Martinez, Cash, Konsa, Pau, Maatsen, Kamara, Barkley, Bailey, McGinn, Rogers, Watkins.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa have given Unai Emery what Arsenal refused to
arts.sum: Appointed 25 months months ago by Nassef Sawiris, Emery has transformed Villa on the pitch, helping them return to European football in 2022/23, before then reaching the Europa Conference League semi-finals and qualifying for Champions League football last term with a fourth-placed finish. Then leading the Yellows to the Champions League semi-final in 2021/22, Emery once again proved himself as one of the top coaches in European football after being sacked at Arsenal in November 2019.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery confirms January transfer dilemma after summer Aston Villa arrival
arts.sum: Ahead of Villa's Premier League clash with Southampton on Saturday, Emery admitted that some of his players are still adapting to his tactical demands. Aston Villa manager Unai Emery will weigh up the possibility of sending Kosta Nedeljkovic out on loan in January.

arts.ttl: FA Youth Cup: Aston Villa 6-0 Accrington Stanley
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa U18 Vs. Accrington Stanley U18 - Fri, Dec 6th 2024, 19:00 Aston Villa opened up their FA Youth Cup campaign with a comprehensive 6-0 victory over Accrington Stanley at Bodymoor Heath. Dominant throughout, Jimmy Shan’s side took a three-goal lead into the half-time break, thanks to strikes from Ben Broggio, Mason Cotcher and Rodrigo Fortes, as Accrington were reduced to 10 players. Another red card after the break made Accrington’s task harder, sandwiched in between a brace of goals from the impressive George Hemmings, before Luka Lynch put the cherry on the cake in stoppage time. The Young Lions stated their intentions from the beginning, as a quick start saw the lively Broggio send a swerving effort narrowly over from the edge of the area, before Cotcher struck the near post minutes later after neat work from Jamal Jimoh-Aloba. Bradley Burrowes was then denied superbly from close range by James Rogerson, after Fortes and Broggio had led a counter-attack, before Villa finally found their breakthrough midway through the opening period. More good play from Jimoh-Aloba on the right-hand side saw the tricky number 10 make his way to the byline before pulling back to the far post, where a waiting Broggio had the simple task of tapping into an empty net. Broggio went close to doubling his and his side’s advantage five minutes later, when his superb effort from range rattled the crossbar, before Accrington’s challenge was made even greater on the half-hour, Rio Alston being shown a straight red card for lashing out at Aidan Borland. And it didn’t take the Villans – against 10 players – long to assert a commanding advantage in the game. Five minutes later, Borland’s quick thinking enabled him to release Mason Cotcher, who made no mistake when one on one for 2-0, despite Tom Bentley-Waite’s best efforts to thwart him. Leon Routh, fresh from penning his first professional contract this week, nodded Borland’s ball into the box inches wide moments later, before Villa added another as half-time approached, to take a three-goal lead into the break. As Rogerson rose to collect Jimoh-Aloba’s deflected effort, he collided with his own defender, spilling the ball into the path of Rodrigo Fortes, who turned into the empty net on the half-volley. The second period brought more of the same from the hosts’ point of view, and within 10 minutes of the restart, they had four, as Jimoh-Aloba fed Broggio on the counter, and he pulled across goal for Hemmings who made no mistake to tap home. Stanley’s night went from bad to worse just after the hour, when they were reduced to nine players after Bentley-Waite was shown a second yellow for a late lunge on Cotcher. Still, Villa weren’t done. Burrowes’ effort from close range shaved the post when he was in the clear, before Hemmings was the next to strike the woodwork, firing against the bar from inside the area after a goalmouth scramble. But Hemmings wouldn’t be denied his second, notching the Young Lions’ fifth of the evening with 10 minutes to play, with the goal of the evening. Receiving the ball on the edge of the area, he danced into the box through the away defence and wrongfooted Rogerson with a confident finish. The cherry was placed on the cake in stoppage time, as substitutes Trai-Varn Mulley and Luka Lynch combined, the former squaring for the latter who walked the ball into the empty net to complete the scoring. The sixth goal rounded off a confident, assured performance from Shan’s side, as a successful night saw them book their place in the fourth round of the FA Youth Cup. Aston Villa: Proctor; Burgess (c), Carroll, Routh, Fortes; Hemmings, Borland (Jenner 56); Burrowes (Quinn 70), Jimoh-Aloba (Brannigan 56), Broggio (Mulley 56); Cotcher (Lynch 66). Subs (not used): Asemota (GK); Wilson. Accrington: Rogerson; Broughton (Kemp 60), Rhodes, Bentley-Waite, Williams, Tunstall (c), Alston, Sohna (Osamuyimen 46), Warbrick, Hanson (Robinson 60), Popoola (Al-Abbas 60). Subs (not used): Knight (GK); Butler, Dias. Upcoming Fixtures West Bromwich Albion U18 Vs. Aston Villa U18 - Sat, Feb 2nd 2025, 12:00 Aston Villa U18 Vs. Reading U18 - Sat, Feb 2nd 2025, 12:00 Norwich City U18 Vs. Aston Villa U18 - Sat, Feb 2nd 2025, 11:00

arts.ttl: I was kept alive by Aston Villa and I'm paying them back
arts.sum: I’ve been writing books chronicling the history of Aston Villa for more than 15 years now - and it’s my greatest pleasure. Sammy Morgan, one of my seventies football heroes - older fans will recall him and his classic chant ‘Six foot two, eyes of blue, Sammy Morgan's after you…' I remember telling Sammy about one particular time when it was really rough, but I thought ‘If I do something I won't know how Villa get on against Coventry next week’.

arts.ttl: Mings, injuries and 'impossible' Villa challenge - inside Unai Emery's Southampton press conference - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: “I appreciate a lot some teams, some young coaches, how they are being brave, playing, showing different ways tactically. For the supporters, for the players, for the team, with the work we have, the coaches and everybody, it was very important.

arts.ttl: Villa great Shaw died from injury in fall - inquest
arts.sum: Aston Villa legend Gary Shaw died from a severe head injury as a result of a fall, an inquest hears.

arts.ttl: Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Southampton
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Southampton - Sat, Dec 7th 2024, 15:00 Aston Villa welcome Southampton to Villa Park on Saturday afternoon, in what will be their third Premier League fixture in the space of seven days. Unai Emery's side take on the Saints looking for back-to-back victories after a 3-1 win over Brentford on Wednesday evening. Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit on matchday... Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets A limited number of general admission tickets for the match remain. Book online here. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of new hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information and book online here. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 1.30pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Parking and Travel Supporters are advised that the supply of car parking around Villa Park is limited. Fans can pre-book their space via either of the links below: www.astonvillaparking.co.uk/football/events www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk We will continue to work with local parking operators to offer fans the opportunity to book parking near the stadium. We would strongly encourage all supporters who have not pre-booked their car parking to travel to Villa Park by alternative modes. Covered cycle parking is available at our Inner City Academy on Witton Road. Plan ahead and check train timetables for direct services or travel earlier for less busy services. Visit Transport for West Midlands’ Journey Planner or the National Rail Enquiries real-time journey planner here. There are a number of Park and Ride sites across the region, for further details visit Park and Ride. To help you plan your journey by public transport, visit How to get to Villa Park. There is no planned disruption to key road, rail and bus routes to Villa Park on Saturday. Traffic signals have been optimised around Villa Park to help improve flow of traffic away from the ground. This should assist with journey times for our AV82 city centre shuttle bus (see below). Supporters are also advised that the city centre is likely to be busier than normal, as the Christmas Market is now live. City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. Traffic signals have been optimised around Villa Park to help improve flow of traffic away from the ground. This should assist with journey times for our AV82 city centre shuttle bus. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Cycle Parking Covered and secure cycle parking is available a short walk away from Villa Park at our Inner City Academy, Brook Vale, on Witton Road. Accessibility information Supporters with accessibility requirements travelling to Villa Park by rail are advised to look out for the accessibility signage on both platforms at Witton Station. Station staff will be able to help you if you wait in the designated areas. More information is available here. For any supporters with additional mobility needs who have questions about travel to and from Villa Park on a matchday, please contact accessibility@avfc.co.uk. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. Enjoy the Fan Zone Villa Park's new Fan Zone is again open on Saturday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doors at 12.30pm, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area and food. The Fan Zone is also in prime position to watch player arrivals from 1.30pm. We also have entertainment taking place on the stage opposite Trinity Reception, including live music. We want to see your experience of matchday at Villa Park! Post your photos and tag #UTV and you could be featured on the big screens at the stadium! Supporters are advised that the R44 accessible entrance will be closed for a period of time. Family Fun Zone Work is soon to begin on the transformation of the existing Academy building at Villa Park. Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 12noon. Supporters are reminded that Aston Villa adopts a one-cup system at Villa Park, in our continued environmental sustainability efforts. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. The Warehouse Design Update The Warehouse, which will now be 40,000 sqft, has been further developed with an enhanced design to accommodate live music and entertainment events for 3,500+ visitors and fans. Structural alterations to over half the rooftop will make way for a newly raised flat roof which will accommodate a generous mezzanine floor and viewing galley from above for both live music and fan zone entertainment. Creating a multi-use space called ‘The Warehouse’, will include a diverse range of Food & Beverage offers and pre- and post-match and event entertainment. The Warehouse will become a vibrant and ‘go to’ destination for the community embedded in the Villa Park and Witton Street scene. Matchday Programme The latest edition of the Villa News & Record will be available to purchase at Saturday's match! Fans can continue their sticker album collection, while the programme is also packed with features and news from around the club. Supporters will be able to purchase copies from sellers at Villa Park on matchday, while they're also available to purchase in the club shop and online here. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9.30am-3pm and 5pm-6pm Holte Store: 12pm-3pm and 5pm-6pm 82 Lounge: 12pm-3pm and 5pm-7pm Lower Grounds: 12pm-3pm and 5pm-7pm Trinity Store: 1.30pm-3pm and for 30 minutes at half-time Click & Collect Don’t miss out on the matchday bundle. You can place your merch orders now and simply Click & Collect on matchday! Pre-order your items and collect at the Villa Store, Holte Store, Holte Suite or Hospitality 82 on matchday. Please note, the Click & Collect order option will be turned off at half-time, but any orders placed up to then will be available to collect until that retail unit closes. Click & Collect here. Winter Warmers Prepare for another chilly afternoon at Villa Park against Southampton with your essential winter warmers! Villa kicked off the festive period with a big three points on Wednesday night, and will be hoping to deliver another Christmas gift on Saturday. Those heading to the game this weekend can warm up for the occasion, with our winter warmers range available in the Villa Store. Scarves, hats, Christmas jumpers and more are sure to be a great investment this winter. Supporters can also take advantage of 30% off pyjamas in our matchday deal. Available in the Villa Store on Saturday only. Supporters are also reminded that our next home match against Manchester City, on Saturday, December 21, will be our ‘Christmas Jumper game’, so get them while you still can! National Christmas Jumper day is also next week too, so this is the weekend to buy! During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here. AV150 Legacy Wall Aston Villa is excited to reveal a new AV150 Legacy Wall at Villa Park as part of our 150th anniversary celebrations. Supporters have the opportunity to become part of our iconic home, wherever you are in the world, by purchasing your very own personalised plaque at Villa Park. Our Legacy Wall is a place where you, your friends and family can show your support for the club, celebrate births, marriages, anniversaries or pay tribute to loved ones. For more information, click here.

arts.ttl: John McGinn smirks as he reveals outrageous tattoo forfeit in cheeky Fantasy Premier League bet
arts.sum: The Aston Villa man bared all as he took part in a British Sign Language series for TNT Sport
